24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:ab_setup}
26	
27	A regular projective surface \(S\) over a field --- such as \(\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1\)
28	or the product \(C \times C\) of a smooth projective curve \(C\) with itself --- has a regular
29	local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension \(2\). To extend a rational map
30	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from \(S\) to an abelian variety across a codimension-\(2\) closed
31	point, one argues that the local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) at such a point has \emph{depth at
32	least} \(2\), hence a rational function with no codimension-\(1\) poles on a neighbourhood of \(x\)
33	extends across \(x\). The depth \(\geq 2\) inequality is exactly what the
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring \(R\) one has \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\),
35	so
36	\[
37	  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
38	\]
39	trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
40	regular local ring of dimension \(d\) admits an \(R\)-regular sequence of length \(d\) generating
41	the maximal ideal, one obtains \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\), i.e.\ \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay
42	(Corollary~REF below). This is the input A.4.a needs.
43	
44	This chapter packages:
45	\begin{enumerate}
46	  \item the regular-sequence definition of depth and its \(\text{Ext}\) reformulation
47	    (Definition~REF and Lemma~REF),
48	  \item the projective-dimension invariant of a finitely-generated module
49	    (Definition~REF),
50	  \item the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma
51	    (Lemma~REF),
52	  \item the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula proper
53	    (Theorem~REF), and
54	  \item the corollary regular \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay
55	    (Corollary~REF).
56	\end{enumerate}
57	A short closing paragraph (Section~REF) explains how A.4.a
58	consumes Corollary~REF to push a codim-1 extension across a
59	codim-2 point.
60	
61	\section{Depth of a module over a local ring}
62	\label{sec:ab_depth}
63	
64	\begin{definition}
65	
66	\leanok
67	  [Depth of a module]
68	  \label{def:depth}
69	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth}
70	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth)
71	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17748-L17759).
72	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
73	  % "Let \(R\) be a ring, and \(I \subset R\) an ideal. Let \(M\) be a finite \(R\)-module.
74	  %  The {\it \(I\)-depth} of \(M\), denoted \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is defined as follows:
75	  %  \begin{enumerate}
76	  %  \item if \(IM \not = M\), then \(\text{depth}_I(M)\) is the supremum in
77	  %  \(\{0, 1, 2, \ldots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences in \(I\),
78	  %  \item if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\).
79	  %  \end{enumerate}
80	  %  If \((R, \mathfrak m)\) is local we call \(\text{depth}_{\mathfrak m}(M)\) simply
81	  %  the {\it depth} of \(M\)."
82	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth).}
83	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(I \subseteq R\) be an ideal, and let \(M\) be a finitely
84	  generated \(R\)-module. The \emph{\(I\)-depth} of \(M\), written \(\text{depth}_I(M)\), is the
85	  supremum in \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths of \(M\)-regular sequences contained
86	  in \(I\), provided \(IM \neq M\); if \(IM = M\) we set \(\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty\). When
87	  \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate
88	  \(\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak{m}}(M)\) and call this simply the
89	  \emph{depth} of \(M\). The depth of the ring \(R\) itself is \(\text{depth}(R)\), the depth
90	  of \(R\) as a module over itself.
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	Recall that a sequence \(f_1, \dots, f_r \in R\) is called \emph{\(M\)-regular} if
94	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_r)M \neq 0\) and for each \(i\), the element \(f_i\) is a non-zero-divisor on
95	\(M/(f_1, \dots, f_{i-1})M\). The empty sequence is regular on \(M\) iff \(M \neq 0\) (it is
96	\emph{not} regular on the zero module, but the convention \(\text{depth}(0) = \infty\)
97	above is the usual one).
98	
99	The two practical computations of depth are the regular-sequence definition (used to
100	establish a \emph{lower} bound) and the \(\text{Ext}\) characterisation (used to establish an
101	\emph{upper} bound and to prove the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequality).
102	
103	\begin{lemma}
104	
105	\leanok
106	  [Depth via \(\text{Ext}\)]
107	  \label{lem:depth_via_ext}
108	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index}
109	  
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext)
111	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17845-L17885).
112	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
113	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\).
114	  %  Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module. Then \(\text{depth}(M)\)
115	  %  is equal to the smallest integer \(i\) such that
116	  %  \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\) is nonzero."
117	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext).}
118	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring with residue field
119	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak{m}\) and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely generated \(R\)-module. Then
120	  \[
121	    \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \inf\bigl\{\, i \in \mathbb{N} \;\bigm|\; \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \,\bigr\}.
122	  \]
123	\end{lemma}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
126	% "Let \(\delta(M)\) denote the depth of \(M\) and let \(i(M)\) denote
127	%  the smallest integer \(i\) such that \(\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)\)
128	%  is nonzero. We will see in a moment that \(i(M) < \infty\).
129	%  By Lemma REF we have
130	%  \(\delta(M) = 0\) if and only if \(i(M) = 0\), because
131	%  \(\mathfrak m \in \text{Ass}(M)\) exactly means
132	%  that \(i(M) = 0\). Hence if \(\delta(M)\) or \(i(M)\) is \(> 0\), then we may
133	%  choose \(x \in \mathfrak m\) such that (a) \(x\) is a nonzerodivisor
134	%  on \(M\), and (b) \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta(M) - 1\).
135	%  Consider the long exact sequence
136	%  of Ext-groups associated to the short exact sequence
137	%  \(0 \to M \to M \to M/xM \to 0\) by Lemma REF:
138	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
139	%  Since \(x \in \mathfrak m\) all the maps $\Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)
140	%  \to \Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)$ are zero, see
141	%  Lemma REF.
142	%  Thus it is clear that \(i(M/xM) = i(M) - 1\). Induction on
143	%  \(\delta(M)\) finishes the proof."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  
146	  
147	\leanok
148	  Write \(\delta := \text{depth}(M)\) and \(i := \inf\{ j : \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \}\).
149	  The base case \(\delta = 0\) is exactly \(\text{Hom}_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0\) (some element of
150	  \(M\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\), i.e.\ \(\mathfrak{m} \in \text{Ass}(M)\), i.e.\
151	  \(\mathfrak{m}\) contains a zero-divisor on \(M\)), which is \(i = 0\). For the inductive step
152	  pick \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) a non-zero-divisor on \(M\) with
153	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta - 1\). The short exact sequence
154	  \(0 \to M \xrightarrow{x} M \to M/xM \to 0\) induces a long exact sequence of $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa,
155	  -)\(. Multiplication by \)x\( annihilates each \)\text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ (because
156	  \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\) and \(\kappa\) is annihilated by \(\mathfrak{m}\)), so the long exact
157	  sequence breaks into short pieces giving
158	  \(\text{Ext}^{j-1}_R(\kappa, M/xM) \cong \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)\) at the relevant
159	  indices, and the smallest non-vanishing index drops by \(1\). By induction
160	  \(i(M/xM) = i - 1 = \delta - 1\), so \(i = \delta\).
161	\end{proof}
162	
163	\section{Projective dimension}
164	\label{sec:ab_pd}
165	
166	\begin{definition}
167	
168	\leanok
169	  [Projective dimension]
170	  \label{def:projective_dimension}
171	  \lean{Module.projectiveDimension}
172	  % NOTE: Mathlib pinned at b80f227 may already expose this as
173	  % `Module.projectiveDimension` via
174	  % `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension` /
175	  % `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution`; the prover should re-export
176	  % rather than re-define if the API is present. This block fixes the
177	  % mathematical content the prover must match.
178	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. The \emph{projective
179	  dimension} of \(M\), written \(\text{pd}_R(M)\), is the infimum in
180	  \(\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}\) of the lengths \(n\) of projective resolutions
181	  \[
182	    0 \longrightarrow P_n \longrightarrow P_{n-1} \longrightarrow \cdots
183	    \longrightarrow P_1 \longrightarrow P_0 \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
184	  \]
185	  with each \(P_i\) a projective \(R\)-module. We say \(M\) has \emph{finite projective dimension}
186	  if \(\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty\), and \emph{infinite projective dimension} otherwise. By
187	  convention \(\text{pd}_R(0) = -\infty\) (some references); we use \(\text{pd}_R(0) = 0\) here
188	  (the empty resolution \(0 \to 0\) of length \(0\)), but the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula in
189	  Theorem~REF concerns only nonzero \(M\).
190	  In the local Noetherian setting a finitely generated \(R\)-module of finite projective
191	  dimension admits a \emph{minimal finite free resolution}: a resolution with each \(P_i\)
192	  free of finite rank and each transition map \(P_{i+1} \to P_i\) landing in \(\mathfrak{m} P_i\).
193	\end{definition}
194	
195	The two basic facts about \(\text{pd}_R(M)\) needed downstream are:
196	\begin{itemize}
197	  \item \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) iff \(M\) is projective (in the local Noetherian setting,
198	    iff \(M\) is free);
199	  \item if \(0 \to K \to P \to M \to 0\) is a short exact sequence with \(P\) projective, then
200	    \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_R(K) + 1\) provided \(K \neq 0\).
201	\end{itemize}
202	Both are standard homological algebra and are recorded under
203	\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension\_succ\_iff} or similar in Mathlib.
204	
205	\section{Depth on a short exact sequence}
206	\label{sec:ab_depth_ses}
207	
208	\begin{lemma}
209	
210	\leanok
211	  [Depth on a short exact sequence]
212	  \label{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
213	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact}
214	  
215	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses)
216	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17887-L17920).
217	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
218	  % "Let \(R\) be a local Noetherian ring. Let \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\)
219	  %  be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite \(R\)-modules.
220	  %  \begin{enumerate}
221	  %  \item
222	  %  \(\text{depth}(N) \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N'), \text{depth}(N'')\}\)
223	  %  \item
224	  %  \(\text{depth}(N'') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N') - 1\}\)
225	  %  \item
226	  %  \(\text{depth}(N') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N'') + 1\}\)
227	  %  \end{enumerate}"
228	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses).}
229	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let
230	  \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of nonzero finitely generated
231	  \(R\)-modules. Then
232	  \begin{enumerate}
233	    \item \(\text{depth}(N) \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N'),\; \text{depth}(N'')\bigr)\);
234	    \item \(\text{depth}(N'') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N') - 1\bigr)\);
235	    \item \(\text{depth}(N') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N'') + 1\bigr)\).
236	  \end{enumerate}
237	\end{lemma}
238	
239	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
240	% "Use the characterization of depth using the Ext groups
241	%  \(\Ext^i(\kappa, N)\), see Lemma REF,
242	%  and use the long exact cohomology sequence
243	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
244	%  from Lemma REF."
245	\begin{proof}
246	  
247	  
248	\leanok
249	  By Lemma~REF, \(\text{depth}(N')\) (resp.\ \(\text{depth}(N)\),
250	  \(\text{depth}(N'')\)) is the smallest index at which \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\) is
251	  nonzero on \(N'\) (resp.\ \(N\), \(N''\)). Apply the long exact \(\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)\)
252	  sequence to \(0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0\):
253	  \[
254	    \cdots \to \text{Ext}^{i-1}_R(\kappa, N'')
255	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N')
256	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N)
257	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N'')
258	    \to \text{Ext}^{i+1}_R(\kappa, N') \to \cdots
259	  \]
260	  Each of the three inequalities is then a direct read-off of the fact that if two
261	  of the three modules in a length-3 chunk vanish then the middle vanishes (for (1)) /
262	  the boundary terms vanish (for (2),(3)). Compare with the diagonal vanishing argument in
263	  the proof of Lemma~REF.
264	\end{proof}

266	\section{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula}
267	\label{sec:ab_main}
268	
269	\begin{theorem}
270	
271	\leanok
272	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum formula]
273	  \label{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
274	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula}
275	  
276	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum)
277	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L27207-L27280).
278	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
279	  % "Let \(R\) be a Noetherian local ring. Let \(M\) be a nonzero finite \(R\)-module
280	  %  which has finite projective dimension \(\text{pd}_R(M)\). Then we have
281	  %  \(\)
282	  %  \text{depth}(R) = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)
283	  %  \(\)"
284	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum); cf.\
285	  Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Theorem 19.1; Auslander--Buchsbaum,
286	  \emph{Homological dimension in local rings}, 1957.}
287	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring and let \(M\) be a nonzero finitely
288	  generated \(R\)-module of finite projective dimension. Then
289	  \[
290	    \text{pd}_R(M) \;+\; \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \text{depth}(R).
291	  \]
292	\end{theorem}
293	
294	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
295	% "We prove this by induction on \(\text{depth}(M)\). The most interesting
296	%  case is the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\). In this case, let
297	%  \(\)
298	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
299	%  \(\)
300	%  be a minimal finite free resolution, so \(e = \text{pd}_R(M)\).
301	%  By Lemma REF we may assume all matrix
302	%  coefficients of the maps in the complex are contained in the maximal
303	%  ideal of \(R\). Then on the one hand, by
304	%  Proposition REF we see that
305	%  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, breaking the long
306	%  exact sequence into short exact sequences
307	%  \begin{align*}
308	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e - 1}} \to K_{e - 2} \to 0,\\
309	%  0 \to K_{e - 2} \to R^{n_{e - 2}} \to K_{e - 3} \to 0,\\
310	%  \ldots,\\
311	%  0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
312	%  \end{align*}
313	%  we see, using Lemma REF, that
314	%  \begin{align*}
315	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1,\\
316	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2,\\
317	%  \ldots,\\
318	%  \text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e - 1),\\
319	%  \text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e
320	%  \end{align*}
321	%  and since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we conclude \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\).
322	%  This finishes the proof of the case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).
323	%
324	%  Induction step. If \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), then we pick \(x \in \mathfrak m\)
325	%  which is a nonzerodivisor on both \(M\) and \(R\). This is possible, because
326	%  either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\) and \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the aforementioned
327	%  Proposition REF or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which
328	%  case \(M\) is finite free hence also \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\).
329	%  Thus \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by Lemma REF
330	%  (for example) and we can find an \(x \in \mathfrak m\) which is a nonzerodivisor
331	%  on both \(R\) and \(M\). Let
332	%  \(\)
333	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
334	%  \(\)
335	%  be a minimal resolution as above. An application of the snake lemma
336	%  shows that
337	%  \(\)
338	%  0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to
339	%  M/xM \to 0
340	%  \(\)
341	%  is a minimal resolution too. Thus \(\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\).
342	%  By Lemma REF we have
343	%  \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
344	%  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\).
345	%  Till now depths have all been depths as \(R\) modules, but we observe that
346	%  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) and similarly for \(R/xR\).
347	%  By induction hypothesis we see that the
348	%  Auslander-Buchsbaum formula holds for \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\). Since the
349	%  depths of both \(R/xR\) and \(M/xM\) have decreased by one and the projective
350	%  dimension has not changed we conclude."
351	\begin{proof}
352	  
353	  We induct on \(\text{depth}(M)\).
354	
355	  \emph{Base case \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\).} Let \(e := \text{pd}_R(M)\) and pick a minimal
356	  finite free resolution
357	  \[
358	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
359	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
360	  \]
361	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in \(\mathfrak{m}\) (this is the
362	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
363	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
364	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
365	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of \(r\)-minors) gives
366	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq e\). On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
367	  sequences
368	  \[
369	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
370	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
371	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
372	  \]
373	  and applying Lemma~REF iteratively,
374	  yields the chain of inequalities
375	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1\),
376	  \(\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2\), \dots,
377	  \(\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)\), and finally
378	  \(\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e\). Since \(\text{depth}(M) = 0\) we deduce
379	  \(\text{depth}(R) \leq e\). Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
380	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
381	
382	  \emph{Inductive step \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\).} We claim there exists \(x \in \mathfrak{m}\)
383	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both \(R\) and \(M\). Indeed: either \(\text{pd}_R(M) > 0\), in
384	  which case \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
385	  of a minimal resolution, or \(\text{pd}_R(M) = 0\) in which case \(M\) is finite free and
386	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0\). Either way both \(\text{depth}(R) > 0\) and
387	  \(\text{depth}(M) > 0\), hence \(\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0\) by
388	  Lemma~REF, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
389	  \mathfrak{m}\( exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of \)M$ as above; the snake
390	  lemma applied to multiplication by \(x\) gives a minimal finite free resolution
391	  \[
392	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
393	  \]
394	  of \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\), of the same length \(e\). So
395	  \(\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)\). By Stacks
396	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both \(\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\) and
397	  \(\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1\), and one verifies
398	  \(\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)\) (regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}\)
399	  correspond to regular sequences in \(\mathfrak{m}/(x)\)). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
400	  \(M/xM\) over \(R/xR\):
401	  \[
402	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
403	  \]
404	  Substituting yields
405	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1\), i.e.\
406	  \(\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)\), as required.
407	\end{proof}

409	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
410	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
411	
412	\begin{definition}
413	
414	\leanok
415	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
416	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
417	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
418	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
419	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
420	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
421	  % "A Noetherian local ring \(R\) is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
422	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
423	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
424	  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
425	  \(\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)\) (equivalently, if \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
426	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite \(R\)-module \(M\) means
427	  \(\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)\), cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
428	\end{definition}
429	
430	% NOTE (iter-185 review, supersedes iter-184): the Lean assembly
431	% `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` calls a non-private helper
432	% `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
433	% L1096 — line drifted from iter-184's L944 reference) that now carries a
434	% structural induction scaffold via `regularLocal_inductive_step`, with the
435	% load-bearing sorry pushed into two named helpers: (i)
436	% `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` consolidating the
437	% Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate gaps in one signature, and (ii) an inline
438	% technical-bridge sorry inside `regularLocal_inductive_step` for the
439	% `R⧸(x)`-vs-`QuotSMulTop x R` linear-equiv upgrade (~10-20 LOC iter-186 close
440	% via `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` + `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`).
441	% Neither has a `\lean{...}` pin in this chapter (the lean-vs-blueprint-checker
442	% iter185-auslander minor flagged adding a pin for the public-facing
443	% `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` itself; iter-186+ chapter touch can add
444	% that hint). The Stacks 00NQ Mathlib gap remains the load-bearing substrate;
445	% iter-186+ work paths unchanged: (a) formalise Stacks 00NQ in project
446	% (~300 LOC); (b) Koszul-homology bypass via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`;
447	% (c) Mathlib upstream.
448	\begin{corollary}
449	
450	\leanok
451	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
452	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
453	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
454	  
455	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
456	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
457	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
458	  % "Let \(R\) be a regular local ring and let
459	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) be a minimal set of generators
460	  %  for the maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\). Then
461	  %  \(x_1, \ldots, x_d\) is a regular sequence, and
462	  %  each \(R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)\) is a regular local ring
463	  %  of dimension \(d - c\). In particular \(R\) is Cohen-Macaulay."
464	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
465	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is
466	  regular local of Krull dimension \(d\), then \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\).
467	\end{corollary}
468	
469	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
470	% "Note that \(R/x_1R\) is a Noetherian local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\)
471	%  by Lemma REF with \(x_2, \ldots, x_d\)
472	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
473	%  Since \(R\) is a domain by Lemma REF
474	%  \(x_1\) is a nonzerodivisor."
475	\begin{proof}
476	  
477	  
478	\leanok
479	  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be regular of dimension \(d\) and pick a minimal generating set
480	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) of \(\mathfrak{m}\). By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), \(R\) is
481	  an integral domain, so \(x_1\) is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient \(R/x_1 R\) is a Noetherian
482	  local ring of dimension \(\geq d - 1\) (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
483	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by \(x_2, \dots, x_d\), hence
484	  has dimension exactly \(d - 1\) and is again regular of dimension \(d - 1\). Inducting on \(d\),
485	  \(x_1, \dots, x_d\) is an \(R\)-regular sequence, so
486	  \(\text{depth}(R) \geq d\). The reverse inequality \(\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d\)
487	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
488	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus \(\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)\), i.e.\
489	  \(R\) is Cohen--Macaulay.
490	
491	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
492	  Theorem~REF as follows: take \(M := R\) itself. Then \(M\) is
493	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank \(1\)), so \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\). The
494	  formula gives \(\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)\), a tautology. The non-tautological
495	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension \(d\), every
496	  finitely generated module \(M\) has \(\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d\) (Stacks tag 00O2,
497	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an \(M\) then
498	  relates \(\text{depth}(M)\) to \(d - \text{pd}_R(M)\), which is the form most often used in
499	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
500	  \(R\) Cohen--Macaulay itself.
501	\end{proof}
502	
503	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
504	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
505	
506	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
507	\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from a regular projective surface \(S\) to an abelian variety \(A\)
508	across a closed point \(x \in S\) that is missing from the domain of definition \(U \subseteq S\).
509	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
510	
511	\begin{enumerate}
512	  \item The local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
513	    dimension \(2\) (because \(S\) is regular and \(\dim S = 2\)).
514	  \item By Corollary~REF it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
515	    \(\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2\). Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
516	    \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\)-regular sequence of length \(2\) (any two transverse generators).
517	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S\) on \(U = S \setminus \{x\}\) extend
518	    uniquely across \(x\) because \(H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) and
519	    \(H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
520	    \(\geq 2\) at \(x\) by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
521	  \item For a rational map \(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) with codimension-\(\geq 2\) indeterminacy
522	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-\(\geq 2\) input produces a sheaf-theoretic
523	    extension of \(f\) to all of \(S\): pull back any sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_A\)-affine-locally,
524	    obtain sections over \(U\), and extend across \(x\) by the above.
525	\end{enumerate}
526	
527	So Corollary~REF is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
528	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
529	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~REF and
530	Corollary~REF are formalized in
531	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
532	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.
533	
534	\section{Lean encoding}
535	\label{sec:ab_lean_encoding}
536	
537	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}.
538	Mathlib at the project's pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) already exposes substantial parts
539	of the depth / projective-dimension API:
540	\begin{itemize}
541	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence} provides
542	    \texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular} (definition of \(M\)-regular sequences),
543	    matching Definition~REF.
544	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth} (if present at the pinned commit) provides
545	    \texttt{Module.depth} as the supremum of regular-sequence lengths; the prover
546	    should re-export this as \texttt{RingTheory.Module.depth} rather than re-define.

572	  \item State Theorem~REF as the formula

574	    ``\((R, \mathfrak{m})\) Noetherian local, \(M\) finitely generated, \(\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty\),
575	    \(M \neq 0\)''.
576	  \item Prove it by induction on \(\text{depth}(M)\) following the structure of the
577	    proof of Theorem~REF above, importing the supporting
578	    Lemma~REF from Mathlib's depth API or
579	    proving it in tandem.
580	  \item Export Corollary~REF as

582	    already present) as the consumer-facing statement.
583	\end{enumerate}
584	No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a homological-algebra construction.
585	The chapter is independent of the rest of Route A's geometric infrastructure (cube of
586	abelian varieties, theorem-of-the-square, Albanese universal property), making it a good
587	parallel-work candidate.
588	
589	\section{Out of scope}
590	\label{sec:ab_out_of_scope}
591	
592	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular
593	\(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay corollary as required by A.4.a. The following downstream
594	constructions are explicitly out of scope here:
595	\begin{itemize}
596	  \item \textbf{The A.4.a codim-1-to-codim-2 extension proof itself} --- consumes
597	    Corollary~REF, but the geometric extension argument
598	    lives in its sibling chapter \texttt{Albanese\_CodimExtension.tex} (planned).
599	  \item \textbf{Local-cohomology vanishing \(H^i_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0\) for \(i < \text{depth}\)}
600	    --- a downstream consequence of Stacks tag 0AVF; planned for its own chapter
601	    \texttt{Albanese\_LocalCohomology.tex} or as part of A.4.a directly.
602	  \item \textbf{Global Cohen--Macaulay schemes / global depth} --- this chapter only treats
603	    the local-ring case. The global-to-local reduction is standard but not packaged here.
604	  \item \textbf{General homological-dimension theory (global dimension, finitistic
605	    dimension)} --- only the local-ring projective-dimension invariant appears here.
606	  \item \textbf{Cohen--Macaulay characterisations via \(\omega_R\), dualising complex,
607	    Gorenstein refinement} --- the formula and its immediate Cohen--Macaulay corollary
608	    suffice for A.4.a; the duality side is not needed.
609	\end{itemize}

257	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
258	  · rw [← Cotangent.lift_comp_toCotangent f hf, LinearMap.coe_comp]
259	    exact Function.Surjective.comp h (toCotangent_surjective I)
260	  · dsimp [Cotangent.lift]
261	    exact QuotientAddGroup.lift_surjective_of_surjective _ _ h _
262	
263	end Lift

265	/-- A linear isomorphism between cotangent spaces induced by an equality of ideals. -/
266	@[expose]
267	def equivOfEq (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) :
268	    I.Cotangent ≃ₗ[R] J.Cotangent where
269	  __ := Cotangent.lift (J.toCotangent ∘ₗ LinearEquiv.ofEq I J hIJ) <| fun x y ↦ by
270	    simp [toCotangent_eq_zero, ← hIJ, sq, mul_mem_mul]
271	  invFun := Cotangent.lift (I.toCotangent ∘ₗ LinearEquiv.ofEq J I hIJ.symm) <| fun x y ↦ by
272	    simp [toCotangent_eq_zero, hIJ, sq, mul_mem_mul]
273	  left_inv x := by
274	    subst hIJ
275	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := I.toCotangent_surjective x
276	    simp
277	  right_inv x := by
278	    subst hIJ
279	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := I.toCotangent_surjective x
280	    simp
281	
282	@[simp]
283	lemma equivOfEq_toCotangent (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) (x : I) :
284	    Cotangent.equivOfEq I J hIJ (I.toCotangent x) = J.toCotangent (LinearEquiv.ofEq I J hIJ x) :=
285	  rfl
286	
287	@[simp]
288	lemma equivOfEq_symm (I J : Ideal R) (hIJ : I = J) :
289	    (Cotangent.equivOfEq I J hIJ).symm = Cotangent.equivOfEq J I hIJ.symm :=
290	  rfl
291	
292	end Ideal.Cotangent

354	    ← (map_injective_of_injective (injective_subtype _)).eq_iff, map_span, Set.image_singleton,

356	  exact ⟨fun ⟨x, h⟩ ↦ ⟨_, h⟩, fun ⟨x, h⟩ ↦ ⟨⟨x, h ▸ subset_span (Set.mem_singleton x)⟩, h⟩⟩

361	
362	lemma Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq (surj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap A B))
363	    {I : Ideal B} {J : Ideal A} (eq : I.comap (algebraMap A B) = RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) ⊔ J) :
364	    Function.Surjective (Ideal.mapCotangent J I (Algebra.ofId A B)
365	      (le_of_le_of_eq le_sup_right eq.symm)) := by
366	  intro x
367	  rcases I.toCotangent_surjective x with ⟨x', rfl⟩
368	  rcases Ideal.exists_of_comap_eq_ker_sup _ surj eq x'.2 with ⟨y', mem, hy'⟩
369	  use J.toCotangent ⟨y', mem⟩
370	  simpa using I.toCotangent.congr_arg (SetCoe.ext hy')
371	
372	lemma Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective (surj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap A B))
373	    {I : Ideal B} {J : Ideal A} (eq : I.comap (algebraMap A B) = RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) ⊔ J) :
374	    (Ideal.mapCotangent J I (Algebra.ofId A B) (le_of_le_of_eq le_sup_right eq.symm)).ker =
375	      (Submodule.comap J.subtype ((RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B)) ⊓ J)).map J.toCotangent := by
376	  have eqmap := Ideal.eq_map_of_comap_eq_ker_sup _ surj eq
377	  refine le_antisymm (fun x hx ↦ ?_) ?_
378	  · rcases J.toCotangent_surjective x with ⟨x', hx'⟩
379	    have : Function.Surjective (Algebra.ofId A B) := surj
380	    simp only [← hx', LinearMap.mem_ker, Ideal.mapCotangent_toCotangent,
381	      Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero, eqmap, Algebra.ofId_apply] at hx
382	    rw [← Ideal.map_pow, ← Ideal.mem_comap, Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' _ surj] at hx
383	    rcases Submodule.mem_sup.mp hx with ⟨y, hy, z, hz, hyz⟩
384	    have : y + z ∈ J := by simp [hyz]
385	    have zmemJ := (Ideal.add_mem_iff_right J (Ideal.pow_le_self (by omega) hy)).mp this
386	    have xeq : x = J.toCotangent ⟨z, zmemJ⟩ := by simpa [← hx', J.toCotangent_eq, ← hyz] using hy
387	    rw [xeq]
388	    exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (Submodule.mem_comap.mpr (Ideal.mem_inf.mpr ⟨hz, zmemJ⟩))
389	  · rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, ← LinearMap.ker_comp]
390	    intro x hx
391	    simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.comp_apply, Ideal.mapCotangent_toCotangent]
392	    convert map_zero I.toCotangent
393	    exact (Ideal.mem_inf.mp hx).1

  [inst_3 : AddCommGroup V₂] [inst_4 : Module K V₂] [FiniteDimensional K V] (f : V →ₗ[K] V₂),
  Module.finrank K ↥f.range + Module.finrank K ↥f.ker = Module.finrank K V"},{"name":"LinearMap.rank_range_add_rank_ker","module_name":"Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity","kind":"theorem","type":"∀ {R : Type u_1} {M M₁ : Type u} [inst : Ring R] [inst_1 : AddCommGroup M] [inst_2 : AddCommGroup M₁]
  [inst_3 : Module R M] [inst_4 : Module R M₁] [HasRankNullity.{u, u_1} R] (f : M →ₗ[R] M₁),
  Module.rank R ↥f.range + Module.rank R ↥f.ker = Module.rank R M"},{"name":"LinearMap.rank_le_of_surjective","module_name":"Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Basic","kind":"theorem","type":"∀ {R : Type u} {M M₁ : Type v} [inst : Semiring R] [inst_1 : AddCommMonoid M] [inst_2 : Module R M]
  [inst_3 : AddCommMonoid M₁] [inst_4 : Module R M₁] (f : M →ₗ[R] M₁),
  Function.Surjective ⇑f → Module.rank R M₁ ≤ Module.rank R M"}]}

{"items":[{"name":"Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_encard","kind":"lemma","file":".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Module/SpanRank.lean"},{"name":"Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite","kind":"lemma","file":".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Module/SpanRank.lean"}]}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Wanyi He, Jiedong Jiang, Xuchun Li, Christian Merten, Jingting Wang, Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Data.ENat.Lattice
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Free
10	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.StrongRankCondition
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Finiteness.Ideal
12	
13	/-!
14	# Minimum Cardinality of generating set of a submodule
15	
16	In this file, we define the minimum cardinality of a generating set for a submodule, which is
17	implemented as `spanFinrank` and `spanRank`.
18	`spanFinrank` takes value in `ℕ` and equals `0` when no finite generating set exists.
19	`spanRank` takes value as a cardinal.
20	
21	## Main Definitions
22	
23	* `spanFinrank`: The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a natural
24	  number. If no finite generating set exists, it is defined to be `0`.
25	* `spanRank`: The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a cardinal.
26	* `FG.generators`: For a finitely generated submodule, get a set of generating elements with minimal
27	  cardinality.
28	
29	## Main Results
30	
31	* `FG.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanFinrank` : Any submodule has a generating set of cardinality equal
32	  to `spanRank`.
33	
34	* `rank_eq_spanRank_of_free` : For a ring `R` (not necessarily commutative) satisfying
35	  `StrongRankCondition R`, if `M` is a free `R`-module, then the `spanRank` of `M` equals to the
36	  rank of M.
37	
38	* `rank_le_spanRank` : For a ring `R` (not necessarily commutative) satisfying
39	  `StrongRankCondition R`, if `M` is an `R`-module, then the `spanRank` of `M` is less than or equal
40	  to the rank of M.
41	
42	## Tags
43	submodule, generating subset, span rank
44	
45	## Remark
46	Note that the corresponding API - `Module.rank` is only defined for a module rather than a
47	submodule, so there is some asymmetry here. Further refactoring might be needed if this difference
48	creates a friction later on.
49	-/
50	
51	@[expose] public section
52	
53	namespace Submodule
54	
55	section Defs
56	
57	universe u v
58	
59	variable {R : Type*} {M : Type u} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
60	
61	open Cardinal
62	
63	/-- The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a cardinal. -/
64	noncomputable def spanRank (p : Submodule R M) : Cardinal := ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s
65	
66	/-- The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a natural number. If no finite
67	  generating set exists, the span rank is defined to be `0`. -/
68	noncomputable def spanFinrank (p : Submodule R M) : ℕ := (spanRank p).toNat
69	
70	instance (p : Submodule R M) : Nonempty {s : Set M // span R s = p} := ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩⟩
71	
72	lemma spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_encard (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanRank.toENat =
73	    (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard) := by
74	  rw [spanRank]
75	  apply le_antisymm
76	  · refine le_iInf₂ (fun s hs ↦ ?_)
77	    rw [Set.encard, ENat.card]
78	    exact toENat.monotone' (ciInf_le' _ (⟨s, hs⟩ : {s : Set M // span R s = p}))
79	  · have := congrFun toENat_comp_ofENat.{u}.symm (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard)
80	    rw [id_eq] at this; rw [this]
81	    refine toENat.monotone' (le_ciInf fun s ↦ ?_)
82	    have : ofENat.{u} (⨅ (s' : Set M), ⨅ (_ : span R s' = p), s'.encard) ≤ ofENat s.1.encard :=
83	      ofENatHom.monotone' (le_trans (ciInf_le' _ s.1) (ciInf_le' _ s.2))
84	    apply le_trans this
85	    rw [Set.encard, ENat.card]
86	    exact Cardinal.ofENat_toENat_le _
87	
88	lemma spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_finset_card (p : Submodule R M) :
89	    p.spanRank.toENat = ⨅ (s : {s : Finset M // span R s = p}), (s.1.card : ℕ∞) := by
90	  rw [spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_encard]
91	  rcases eq_or_ne (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard) ⊤ with (h1 | h2)
92	  · rw [h1, eq_comm]; simp_rw [iInf_eq_top] at h1 ⊢
93	    exact fun s ↦ False.elim (Set.encard_ne_top_iff.mpr s.1.finite_toSet (h1 s.1 s.2))
94	  · simp_rw [← Set.encard_coe_eq_coe_finsetCard]
95	    apply le_antisymm
96	    · exact le_iInf fun s ↦ iInf₂_le (s.1 : Set M) s.2
97	    · refine le_iInf fun s ↦ le_iInf fun h ↦ ?_
98	      by_cases hs : s.Finite
99	      · exact iInf_le_of_le ⟨hs.toFinset, by simpa⟩ (by simp)
100	      · rw [Set.Infinite.encard_eq hs]
101	        exact OrderTop.le_top _
102	
103	lemma spanFinrank_eq_iInf (p : Submodule R M) :
104	    p.spanFinrank = ⨅ (s : {s : Finset M // span R s = p}), s.1.card := by
105	  simp [spanFinrank, Cardinal.toNat, spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_finset_card, ENat.iInf_toNat]
106	
107	/-- A submodule's `spanRank` is finite if and only if it is finitely generated. -/
108	@[simp]
109	lemma spanRank_finite_iff_fg {p : Submodule R M} : p.spanRank < aleph0 ↔ p.FG := by
110	  rw [spanRank, Submodule.fg_def]
111	  constructor
112	  · rintro h
113	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ : ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s ∈
114	      Set.range (fun (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}) ↦ #s) := csInf_mem ⟨#p, ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩, rfl⟩⟩
115	    refine ⟨s.1, ?_, s.2⟩
116	    simpa [← hs] using h
117	  · rintro ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩
118	    exact (ciInf_le' _ ⟨s, hs₂⟩).trans_lt (by simpa)
119	
120	lemma spanFinrank_of_not_fg {p : Submodule R M} (hp : ¬p.FG) : p.spanFinrank = 0 := by
121	  refine toNat_eq_zero.2 ?_
122	  right
123	  by_contra! h
124	  exact hp (spanRank_finite_iff_fg.1 h)
125	
126	/-- A submodule is finitely generated if and only if its `spanRank` is equal to its `spanFinrank`.
127	-/
128	lemma fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} : p.spanRank = p.spanFinrank ↔ p.FG := by
129	  rw [spanFinrank, ← spanRank_finite_iff_fg, eq_comm]
130	  exact cast_toNat_eq_iff_lt_aleph0
131	
132	lemma FG.spanRank_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (fg : p.FG) : p.spanRank = p.spanFinrank :=
133	  fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr fg
134	
135	lemma FG.spanRank_le_iff {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) (n : ℕ) :
136	    p.spanRank ≤ n ↔ p.spanFinrank ≤ n :=
137	  (Cardinal.toNat_le_iff_of_lt_aleph0 n (by simpa)).symm
138	
139	lemma FG.spanRank_eq_iff {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) (n : ℕ) :
140	    p.spanRank = n ↔ p.spanFinrank = n :=
141	  (Cardinal.toNat_eq_iff_of_lt_aleph0 n (by simpa)).symm
142	
143	lemma spanRank_span_le_card (s : Set M) : (Submodule.span R s).spanRank ≤ #s := by
144	  rw [spanRank]
145	  let s' : {s1 : Set M // span R s1 = span R s} := ⟨s, rfl⟩
146	  exact ciInf_le' _ s'
147	
148	lemma spanRank_span_range_of_linearIndependent [RankCondition R] {ι : Type u} {v : ι → M}
149	    (hv : v.Injective) (hs : LinearIndependent R v) :
150	    (span R (.range v)).spanRank = #ι := by
151	  refine le_antisymm (le_trans (spanRank_span_le_card _) mk_range_le) (le_ciInf fun x ↦ ?_)
152	  have : #x.1 = #((Subtype.val : span R (.range v) → _) ⁻¹' x.1) :=
153	    (mk_preimage_of_injective_of_subset_range _ _ Subtype.val_injective (by simp [← x.2])).symm
154	  rw [this]
155	  refine le_trans ?_ ((Module.Basis.span hs).le_span (R := R) (J := Subtype.val ⁻¹' x.1) ?_)
156	  · rw [mk_range_eq]
157	    exact .of_comp (f := Subtype.val) (by convert hv; ext; simp [Module.Basis.span_apply])
158	  · apply map_injective_of_injective (f := (span R _).subtype) (injective_subtype _)
159	    simp [map_span, Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, Set.inter_eq_self_of_subset_left, ← x.2]
160	
161	lemma spanRank_span_of_linearIndepOn [RankCondition R] (s : Set M) (hs : LinearIndepOn R id s) :
162	    (span R s).spanRank = #s := by
163	  simp [← spanRank_span_range_of_linearIndependent Subtype.val_injective hs]
164	
165	lemma spanFinrank_span_le_encard (s : Set M) : (span R s).spanFinrank ≤ s.encard := by
166	  rw [spanFinrank, Set.encard, ENat.card]
167	  exact le_trans (by simp) (toENat.monotone' (spanRank_span_le_card (R := R) s))
168	
169	lemma spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite {s : Set M} (hs : s.Finite) :
170	    (span R s).spanFinrank ≤ s.ncard := by
171	  rw [← Nat.cast_le (α := ℕ∞)]
172	  exact le_trans (spanFinrank_span_le_encard _) hs.cast_ncard_eq.ge
173	
174	/-- Constructs a generating set with cardinality equal to the `spanRank` of the submodule -/
175	theorem exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank (p : Submodule R M) :
176	    ∃ s : Set M, #s = p.spanRank ∧ span R s = p := by
177	  rw [spanRank]
178	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ : ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s ∈
179	    Set.range (fun (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}) ↦ #s) := csInf_mem ⟨#p, ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩, rfl⟩⟩
180	  exact ⟨s.1, ⟨hs, s.2⟩⟩
181	
182	/-- Constructs a generating set with cardinality equal to the `spanFinrank` of the submodule when
183	  the submodule is finitely generated. -/
184	theorem FG.exists_span_set_encard_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
185	    ∃ s : Set M, s.encard = p.spanFinrank ∧ span R s = p := by
186	  obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
187	  refine ⟨s, ⟨?_, hs₂⟩⟩
188	  have := fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h
189	  rw [Set.encard, ENat.card, spanFinrank, hs₁, this]
190	  simp
191	
192	/-- Constructs a generating finset with cardinality equal to the `spanFinrank` of the submodule
193	  when the submodule is finitely generated. -/
194	theorem FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
195	    ∃ s : Finset M, s.card = p.spanFinrank ∧ span R s = p := by
196	  obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_encard_eq_spanFinrank h
197	  have s_f := Set.finite_of_encard_eq_coe hs₁
198	  refine ⟨s_f.toFinset, ⟨?_, by simpa using hs₂⟩⟩
199	  simpa [s_f.encard_eq_coe_toFinset_card, ENat.coe_inj] using hs₁
200	
201	lemma lift_spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p : Submodule R M) {a : Cardinal.{max u v}} :
202	    Cardinal.lift.{v} p.spanRank ≤ a ↔ ∃ s : Set M, Cardinal.lift.{v} #s ≤ a ∧ span R s = p := by
203	  constructor
204	  · intro h
205	    obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
206	    exact ⟨s, ⟨hs₁ ▸ h, hs₂⟩⟩
207	  · exact fun ⟨s, ⟨h₁, h₂⟩⟩ ↦ h₂.symm ▸ (Cardinal.lift_le.mpr (spanRank_span_le_card s)).trans h₁
208	
209	/-- For a finitely generated submodule, its spanRank is less than or equal to a cardinal `a`
210	  if and only if there is a generating subset with cardinality less than or equal to `a`. -/
211	lemma FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p : Submodule R M) {a : Cardinal} :
212	    p.spanRank ≤ a ↔ ∃ s : Set M, #s ≤ a ∧ span R s = p := by
213	  convert lift_spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le p (a := a) <;> simp
214	
215	@[simp]
216	lemma spanRank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot {I : Submodule R M} : I.spanRank = 0 ↔ I = ⊥ := by
217	  constructor
218	  · intro h
219	    obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ :=
220	      (FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le I (a := 0)).mp (by rw [h])
221	    simp only [nonpos_iff_eq_zero, mk_eq_zero_iff, Set.isEmpty_coe_sort] at hs₁
222	    simp_all
223	  · rintro rfl; rw [spanRank]
224	    exact Cardinal.iInf_eq_zero_iff.mpr (Or.inr ⟨⟨∅, by simp⟩, by simp⟩)
225	
226	@[simp]
227	lemma spanRank_bot : (⊥ : Ideal R).spanRank = 0 := Submodule.spanRank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot.mpr rfl
228	
229	@[simp]
230	lemma spanFinrank_bot : (⊥ : Submodule R M).spanFinrank = 0 := by simp [spanFinrank]
231	
232	@[nontriviality]
233	lemma spanRank_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanRank = 0 := by
234	  simp [nontriviality]
235	
236	@[nontriviality]
237	lemma spanFinrank_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanFinrank = 0 := by
238	  have := Module.subsingleton R M
239	  simp [Submodule.eq_bot_of_subsingleton]
240	
241	/-- Generating elements for the submodule of minimum cardinality. -/
242	noncomputable def generators (p : Submodule R M) : Set M :=
243	  Classical.choose (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)
244	
245	lemma generators_card (p : Submodule R M) : #(generators p) = spanRank p :=
246	  (Classical.choose_spec (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)).1
247	
248	lemma FG.generators_ncard {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
249	    (generators p).ncard = spanFinrank p := by
250	  rw [← Nat.cast_inj (R := Cardinal), ← fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h, Set.ncard, Set.encard,
251	     ENat.card, generators_card, toNat_toENat, ← spanFinrank]
252	  exact (fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h).symm
253	
254	lemma FG.finite_generators {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) :
255	    p.generators.Finite := by
256	  rw [← Cardinal.lt_aleph0_iff_set_finite, Submodule.generators_card]
257	  exact spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr hp
258	
259	/-- The span of the generators equals the submodule. -/
260	lemma span_generators (p : Submodule R M) : span R (generators p) = p :=
261	  (Classical.choose_spec (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)).2
262	
263	/-- The elements of the generators are in the submodule. -/
264	lemma FG.generators_mem (p : Submodule R M) : generators p ⊆ p := by
265	  nth_rw 2 [← span_generators p]
266	  exact subset_span (s := generators p)
267	
268	lemma spanRank_sup_le_sum_spanRank {p q : Submodule R M} :
269	    (p ⊔ q).spanRank ≤ p.spanRank + q.spanRank := by
270	  apply (FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p ⊔ q)).mpr
271	  obtain ⟨sp, ⟨hp₁, rfl⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
272	  obtain ⟨sq, ⟨hq₁, rfl⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank q
273	  exact ⟨sp ∪ sq, ⟨hp₁ ▸ hq₁ ▸ (Cardinal.mk_union_le sp sq), span_union sp sq⟩⟩
274	
275	lemma spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
276	    p.spanFinrank = 0 ↔ p = ⊥ := by
277	  refine ⟨fun heq ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ by simp [h]⟩
278	  rw [← Submodule.FG.generators_ncard h, Set.ncard_eq_zero h.finite_generators] at heq
279	  rw [← p.span_generators, heq, span_empty]
280	
281	lemma spanFinrank_singleton {m : M} (hm : m ≠ 0) : (span R {m}).spanFinrank = 1 := by
282	  apply le_antisymm ?_ ?_
283	  · exact le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (by simp)) (by simp)
284	  · by_contra!
285	    simp [Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot (fg_span_singleton m), hm] at this
286	
287	end Defs
288	
289	end Submodule
290

300	  {L : Type v} [AddCommMonoid L] [Module S L]

24:* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
93:lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Nailin Guan. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Nailin Guan
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRank
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.Module
11	
12	/-!
13	# Span rank under operations
14	
15	In this file we show how operations on submodules interact with `Submodule.spanRank`.
16	
17	# Main Results
18	
19	* `Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le`: Base change doesn't increase the span rank.
20	
21	* `TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField`: For a finitely generated module over
22	  a local ring, the dimension of the base change to the residue field is equal to its span rank.
23	
24	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
25	  generators of the unique maximal ideal is equal to the dimension of the cotangent space.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open IsLocalRing TensorProduct Submodule
32	
33	variable {R A : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
34	  {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M)
35	
36	lemma Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le : (N.baseChange A).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
37	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := N.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
38	  grw [← hs₁, ← hs₂, baseChange_span, spanRank_span_le_card]
39	  convert Cardinal.mk_image_le_lift (f := TensorProduct.mk R A M 1) (s := s)
40	  · exact (Cardinal.lift_id' _).symm
41	  · exact Cardinal.lift_umax.symm
42	
43	lemma Submodule.FG.spanFinrank_baseChange_le (fg : N.FG) :
44	    (N.baseChange A).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
45	  grw [spanFinrank, spanRank_baseChange_le, Cardinal.toNat_lift, spanFinrank]
46	  simp [Cardinal.lift_lt_aleph0, spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr fg]
47	
48	lemma TensorProduct.spanRank_top_le : (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
49	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanRank_top, spanRank_baseChange_le]
50	
51	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg (fg : N.FG) :
52	    (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
53	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanFinrank_top, (N.fg_top.mpr fg).spanFinrank_baseChange_le]
54	
55	variable [IsLocalRing R]
56	local notation "𝓀" => ResidueField R
57	
58	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField (fg : N.FG) :
59	    (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank = N.spanFinrank := by
60	  let : Module.Finite R N := Module.Finite.iff_fg.mpr fg
61	  apply (TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg N fg).antisymm
62	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
63	  have hs₃ : s.Finite := Cardinal.mk_lt_aleph0_iff.mp (by simpa [hs₁] using Module.Finite.fg_top)
64	  let t := Function.surjInv (mk_surjective R N 𝓀 residue_surjective) '' s
65	  have ht₁ : mk R 𝓀 N 1 '' t = s := by rw [← Set.image_comp, Function.comp_surjInv, s.image_id]
66	  have ht₂ : span R t = ⊤ := by
67	    rwa [← restrictScalars_eq_top_iff R, restrictScalars_span _ _ (by exact residue_surjective),
68	      ← ht₁, ← map_span, map_tensorProduct_mk_eq_top] at hs₂
69	  grw [← N.spanFinrank_top, ← ht₂, spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (hs₃.image _), spanFinrank,
70	    ← hs₁, Set.ncard_image_le hs₃]
71	  rfl
72	
73	namespace IsLocalRing
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	lemma spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient (N : Submodule R M) (fg : N.FG) :
77	    N.spanFinrank =
78	      Module.finrank (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) (N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) := by
79	  let : Module 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
80	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
81	  let : IsScalarTower R 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
82	    inferInstanceAs (IsScalarTower R (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
83	  rw [← spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField N fg, ← Module.finrank_eq_spanFinrank_of_free]
84	  let e : 𝓀 ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[𝓀] N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N) :=
85	    (quotTensorEquivQuotSMul N (maximalIdeal R)).extendScalarsOfSurjective residue_surjective
86	  exact e.finrank_eq
87	
88	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (fg : (maximalIdeal R).FG) :
89	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
90	  spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient _ fg
91	
92	variable (R) in
93	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :
94	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
95	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (maximalIdeal R).fg_of_isNoetherianRing
96	
97	end IsLocalRing
98

input.new_string:
rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R,
      ← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace
          (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})]
  -- Goal: (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (𝔪 R).spanFinrank
  -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1: close via (≥) lift-and-cons + (≤) cotangent
  -- rank-nullity.
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  · -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- **iter-188 progress note**: this is the Nakayama-extension direction.
    -- The substantive cotangent-space rank-nullity argument requires the
    -- κ-vs-κ' residue-field identification (κ = R/𝔪 vs κ' = (R/(x))/𝔪').
    -- The two are canonically isomorphic via `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotSup`
    -- applied at I = (x), J = 𝔪 (gives (R/(x))/(𝔪/(x)) ≃+* R/((x)⊔𝔪) = R/𝔪 = κ).
    -- The κ-linear surjection mapCotangent : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent (via
    -- the iso) is surjective with 1-dim kernel κ-span{x̄}; rank-nullity then
    -- gives the equation. Bookkeeping for the residue-field switch is left
    -- as a typed sorry for iter-189+.
    sorry
  · -- (≥): (𝔪 R).spanFinrank ≤ (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1.
    -- Lift-and-cons strategy: a min gen set T of 𝔪 (R/(x)) lifts to T_lift ⊆
    -- 𝔪 R via `Function.surjInv` of `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the union
    -- `T_lift ∪ {x}` generates 𝔪 R since 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪').
    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
    let g : R' → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
    have hg : ∀ y, mkx (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
    -- Get min gen finset of 𝔪'.
    have h𝔪'_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
    obtain ⟨T, hT_card, hT_span⟩ := h𝔪'_fg.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank
    -- T : Finset R', T.card = spanFinrank 𝔪', span R' T = 𝔪'.
    let T_lift : Finset R := T.image g
    let U : Finset R := insert x T_lift
    -- Step A: U generates 𝔪 R via the comap identification.
    -- Comap mkx (𝔪 R') = 𝔪 R, since R is local and mkx is surjective.
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have hker_eq : RingHom.ker mkx = Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.mk_ker
    -- Step B: 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span T_lift) ⊔ (Ideal.span {x}).
    -- Compute: comap mkx (map mkx (Ideal.span T_lift)) = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ ker mkx.
    have hmap_T_lift : Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' := by
      rw [Ideal.map_span]
      -- mkx '' T_lift = T (as sets).
      have him : mkx '' (T_lift : Set R) = (T : Set R') := by
        ext y
        simp only [Set.mem_image, Finset.coe_image, T_lift]
        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
        · rintro ⟨a, ⟨b, hbT, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
          rw [hg b]; exact hbT
        · intro hyT
          exact ⟨g y, ⟨y, hyT, rfl⟩, hg y⟩
      rw [him]; exact hT_span
    have h𝔪R_decomp : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R =
        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
      calc IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R
          = Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') := hcomap_eq.symm
        _ = Ideal.comap mkx (Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R))) := by
              rw [hmap_T_lift]
        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ RingHom.ker mkx := by
              rw [Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' mkx Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective]
        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by rw [hker_eq]
    -- Step C: span R U = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ Ideal.span {x}.
    have hU_span_eq : Submodule.span R (↑U : Set R) =
        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
      have : (↑U : Set R) = (↑T_lift : Set R) ∪ {x} := by
        simp only [U, Finset.coe_insert, Set.insert_eq, Set.union_comm]
      rw [this, Submodule.span_union]
      rfl
    -- Step D: spanFinrank 𝔪 R ≤ U.card ≤ T.card + 1.
    calc Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
        ≤ (↑U : Set R).ncard := by
          rw [← h𝔪R_decomp, ← hU_span_eq]
          exact Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite U.finite_toSet
      _ = U.card := Set.ncard_coe_Finset _
      _ ≤ T_lift.card + 1 := by
          have := Finset.card_insert_le x T_lift
          simpa [U] using this
      _ ≤ T.card + 1 := by
          have : T_lift.card ≤ T.card := by simp [T_lift, Finset.card_image_le]
          omega
      _ = Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') + 1 := by
          rw [hT_card]

· -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1 partial: cotangent-space rank-nullity
    -- approach. We reduce to the cotangent-space finrank inequality
    -- `finrank κ' (𝔪'.Cotangent) + 1 ≤ finrank κ (𝔪.Cotangent)` via the
    -- spanFinrank-cotangent bridge, then prove it using:
    -- (a) Helper 1.5: x̄ ≠ 0 in 𝔪.Cotangent (axiom-clean, landed iter-187).
    -- (b) R-linear surjection mapCotangent : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent with
    --     kernel = R-span{x̄} = κ-span{x̄} (1-dim κ-subspace).
    -- (c) Rank-nullity over κ on 𝔪.Cotangent quotient by κ-span{x̄}.
    -- (d) κ ≃+* κ' residue-field switch via `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotSup`
    --     applied at I = (x), J = 𝔪 (giving (R/(x))/(𝔪/(x)) ≃+* R/((x)⊔𝔪) = R/𝔪 = κ).
    -- (e) finrank-preservation under the κ ≃+* κ' iso (via
    --     `Algebra.finrank_eq_of_equiv_equiv` or `Module.compHom`).
    --
    -- The structural setup below carries out (a)-(c); the κ → κ' iso (d)
    -- + finrank transfer (e) is the residual narrow named sorry.
    rw [show Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
            = Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
                (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) from
        IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace _,
        show Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
            = Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
                (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) from
        IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace _]
    -- Goal: finrank κ' (CotangentSpace R') + 1 ≤ finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
    -- Helper 1.5 input: x̄ ≠ 0 in 𝔪.Cotangent.
    have hx_bar_ne : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
        ⟨x, hx⟩ ≠ 0 := toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq x hx hxnotsq
    -- iter-189+ residual: the κ → κ' residue-field iso + finrank transfer
    -- bookkeeping. The substantive cotangent-space rank-nullity argument
    -- is structurally complete in the comment above; closing this typed
    -- sorry requires ~50-80 LOC of κ-module/finrank bookkeeping.
    sorry
input.new_string:
· -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1: Steinitz-exchange strategy.
    -- Take a min gen finset V of 𝔪 R of size n. Express x = ∑ cᵥ v in
    -- R-linear combination of V. Since x ∉ 𝔪², some coefficient cᵥ₀ ∉ 𝔪,
    -- hence is a unit (R local). Then v₀ ∈ Ideal.span ({x} ∪ V \ {v₀}),
    -- so 𝔪 R = Ideal.span ({x} ∪ V.erase v₀). Applying mkx (which kills x),
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)), a set
    -- of cardinality ≤ n - 1.
    classical
    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
    -- Get min gen finset V of 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
    obtain ⟨V, hV_card, hV_span⟩ :=
      Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h𝔪_fg
    -- Step 1: spanFinrank 𝔪 R ≥ 1 (since x ∉ 𝔪² implies x ≠ 0, hence 𝔪 ≠ ⊥).
    have h_n_ge_1 : 1 ≤ Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      rw [← hV_card]
      by_contra h
      push_neg at h
      have hV_empty : V.card = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp h
      have hV_eq : V = ∅ := Finset.card_eq_zero.mp hV_empty
      have h𝔪_bot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R = ⊥ := by
        rw [← hV_span, hV_eq, Finset.coe_empty, Submodule.span_empty]
      apply hxnotsq
      rw [h𝔪_bot]; simp
    -- Step 2: x ∈ Submodule.span R V, extract coefficients via mem_span_finset.
    have hx_mem : x ∈ Submodule.span R (V : Set R) := hV_span ▸ hx
    obtain ⟨c, _hc_supp, hc_sum⟩ := Submodule.mem_span_finset.mp hx_mem
    -- Step 3: ∃ v₀ ∈ V with c v₀ ∉ 𝔪 R (i.e., c v₀ is a unit).
    -- If all c v ∈ 𝔪, then x = Σ c v • v ∈ 𝔪 · 𝔪 = 𝔪². Contradicts hxnotsq.
    have hexists_unit : ∃ v₀ ∈ V, c v₀ ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      by_contra h
      push_neg at h
      apply hxnotsq
      rw [pow_two, ← hc_sum]
      refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
      intro v hvV
      have hcv_mem : c v ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := h v hvV
      have hv_mem : v ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        rw [← hV_span]; exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hvV)
      -- c v • v = c v * v ∈ 𝔪 * 𝔪 = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
      have : c v • v ∈ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) *
          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        rw [smul_eq_mul]
        exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul hcv_mem hv_mem
      simpa [Ideal.smul_eq_mul] using this
    obtain ⟨v₀, hv₀_V, hv₀_notmem⟩ := hexists_unit
    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hv₀_unit
    -- u : Rˣ with ↑u = c v₀.
    -- Step 4: 𝔪 R = Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
    -- We show: v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
    have hv₀_in_new : v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      -- v₀ = u⁻¹ · (x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v)
      -- because c v₀ * v₀ = x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v (from hc_sum).
      have hsum_split : c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v • v = x := by
        rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
        simp [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
        linarith_or_polyrith
      sorry  -- TODO: extract v₀ from the equation
    -- Step 5: span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = span V.
    -- One direction: V ⊆ span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) — for v = v₀, use hv₀_in_new;
    -- for v ≠ v₀, v ∈ V.erase v₀ ⊆ insert x (V.erase v₀).
    -- Other direction: insert x (V.erase v₀) ⊆ 𝔪 = span V — x ∈ 𝔪 = span V,
    -- V.erase v₀ ⊆ V ⊆ span V.
    sorry

        rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
        simp only [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
        linarith
      -- So v₀ = u⁻¹ * x + Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} (-(u⁻¹ * c v)) * v.
      have hu_inv : (u⁻¹ : R) * c v₀ = 1 := by
        rw [← hu]; exact Units.inv_mul u
      have hv₀_eq : v₀ = (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x +
          ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (-(↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v) * v := by
        have : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * (c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v) =
              (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x := by rw [hsum_split]
        rw [mul_add, ← mul_assoc, hu_inv, one_mul, Finset.mul_sum] at this
        linarith
      rw [hv₀_eq]
      refine Submodule.add_mem _ ?_ (Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_)
      · exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert _ _))
      · intro v hvErase
        refine Submodule.smul_mem _ _ ?_
        exact Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert_of_mem _ (by exact_mod_cast hvErase))
    -- Step 5: span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪R_new : Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · -- insert x (V.erase v₀) ⊆ 𝔪 R: x ∈ 𝔪, V.erase v₀ ⊆ V ⊆ 𝔪.
        rw [Submodule.span_le]
        rintro y hy
        rcases hy with rfl | hy
        · exact hx
        · have hy_V : y ∈ V := Finset.mem_of_mem_erase (by exact_mod_cast hy)
          rw [← hV_span]
          exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hy_V)
      · -- 𝔪 R = span V ⊆ span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
        rw [← hV_span, Submodule.span_le]
        intro v hv
        by_cases hv_eq : v = v₀
        · rw [hv_eq]; exact hv₀_in_new
        · refine Submodule.subset_span ?_
          right
          exact_mod_cast Finset.mem_erase.mpr ⟨hv_eq, by exact_mod_cast hv⟩
    -- Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R (image equals 𝔪'); applying Ideal.map_span
    -- and mkx x = 0 collapses to the desired set.
    have h_mkx_x : mkx x = 0 := by
      change (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})) x = 0
      rw [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
      exact Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have h𝔪R'_eq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' =
        Ideal.map mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← hcomap_eq]
      exact (Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).symm
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx (span (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --      = Ideal.span (mkx '' insert x (V.erase v₀))
    --      = Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' V.erase v₀))
    --      = Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀).
    have h𝔪R'_span_image : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' =
        Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      rw [h𝔪R'_eq, ← h𝔪R_new, Ideal.map_span]
      congr 1
      ext y
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_insert_iff]
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · rintro ⟨a, (rfl | haErase), rfl⟩
        · -- mkx a where a = x, so y = mkx x = 0. We want y ∈ mkx '' V.erase v₀.
          -- Since 0 ∈ Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀), this should work by
          -- pulling 0 in. But here we want y to be in the SET. Actually we want
          -- the spans equal. So this `congr 1` may not work — let me re-do.
          sorry
        · exact ⟨a, haErase, rfl⟩
      · rintro ⟨a, haErase, rfl⟩
        exact ⟨a, Or.inr haErase, rfl⟩
    -- Step 7: spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).ncard ≤ V.erase v₀.card = n - 1.
    have h_bound : Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ≤
        V.card - 1 := by
      calc Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')
          ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)).ncard := by
            rw [h𝔪R'_span_image]
            exact Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite
              ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet.image mkx)
        _ ≤ ((V.erase v₀ : Set R).ncard) :=
            Set.ncard_image_le ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet)
        _ = (V.erase v₀).card := by simp
        _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V
    omega

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ (line 984)

**Target (iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1)**: close the spanFinrank dim-drop
equation `(𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (𝔪 R).spanFinrank`.

### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Split into (≥) lift-and-cons strategy + (≤) Steinitz-exchange
  strategy. After the existing rewrites via
  `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, the
  cotangent equation reduces to the spanFinrank equation; we then `le_antisymm`
  into the two inequalities.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — (≥) direction closed kernel-clean (~50 LOC).
  (≤) direction reduced to a much narrower typed sorry (~30-50 LOC away
  from full closure).

### (≥) direction (CLOSED, axiom-clean, ~50 LOC)

**Strategy**: lift a min gen finset `T` of `𝔪 (R/(x))` to `T_lift ⊆ 𝔪 R`
via `Function.surjInv (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective)`; the union
`T_lift ∪ {x}` generates `𝔪 R` since `𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪')`
via `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'`.

**Key Mathlib lemmas used**:
- `Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank` (extract min gen
  finset matching spanFinrank).
- `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'` (`comap f (map f I) = I ⊔ ker f`).
- `IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff` (R local: `IsMaximal I ↔ I = maximalIdeal R`).
- `Ideal.map_span` (image of span equals span of image).
- `Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite` (cardinality bound).

### (≤) direction (PARTIAL, Steps 1-3 axiom-clean ~25 LOC, Steps 4-7 typed sorry)

**Strategy**: Steinitz-exchange. Take min gen finset V of 𝔪 R of size n.
Express x as R-linear combo of V; since `x ∉ 𝔪²`, some coefficient `c v₀ ∉ 𝔪`,
hence is a unit (R local). Then `v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))`,
so `𝔪 R = Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))`. Applying mkx (which kills x),
`𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀))`, a set of cardinality ≤ n - 1.

**Closed steps (Steps 1-3, axiom-clean)**:
1. **Step 1**: `1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank` (via `x ∉ 𝔪² ⇒ x ≠ 0 ⇒ 𝔪 ≠ ⊥`).
2. **Step 2**: extract `c : R → R` with `∑ a ∈ V, c a • a = x` via
   `Submodule.mem_span_finset`.
3. **Step 3 (Nakayama-based unit extraction, axiom-clean)**: `∃ v₀ ∈ V`
   with `c v₀ ∉ 𝔪 R` (hence is a unit by `IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal`).
   By contradiction: if all `c v ∈ 𝔪`, then
   `x = Σ c v • v ∈ 𝔪 * 𝔪 = 𝔪²` via `Ideal.mul_mem_mul`, contradicting `hxnotsq`.
   This is the **substantive ring-theoretic content** of the (≤) direction.

**Residual narrow typed sorry (Steps 4-7, ~30-50 LOC, iter-189+)**:
4. **Step 4**: `v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀))`. Extract via
   the unit `c v₀`: `v₀ = (c v₀)⁻¹ * (x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v * v)`.
5. **Step 5**: `Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R`. Both
   inclusions are straightforward given Step 4 and `hV_span`.

    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    -- Steps 4-7 (residual narrow named sorry, iter-189+):
    --   Step 4: v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) (extract via hv₀_unit).
    --   Step 5: Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    --   Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)) (apply mkx; mkx x = 0).
    --   Step 7: spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |V.erase v₀| = n - 1, conclude via h_n_ge_1.

    -- bookkeeping, no further substrate gaps.

input.new_string:
obtain ⟨v₀, hv₀_V, hv₀_notmem⟩ := hexists_unit
    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hv₀_unit
    -- Step 4: v₀ = ↑u⁻¹ * x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} ↑u⁻¹ * c v * v.
    have hsum_split : c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v = x := by
      rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
      simp only [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
      linear_combination hc_sum
    have hu_inv : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v₀ = 1 := by rw [← hu]; exact Units.inv_mul u
    have h_sum_eq : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v =
        ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v * v := by
      rw [Finset.mul_sum]; apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intros; ring
    have hv₀_eq : v₀ = (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x -
        ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v * v := by
      have h1 : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * (c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v) =
          (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x := by rw [hsum_split]
      rw [mul_add, ← mul_assoc, hu_inv, one_mul, h_sum_eq] at h1
      linear_combination h1
    have hv₀_in_new : v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [hv₀_eq]
      refine Submodule.sub_mem _ ?_ (Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_)
      · exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert _ _))
      · intro v hvErase
        exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert_of_mem _
            (by exact_mod_cast hvErase)))
    -- Step 5: Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪R_new : Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · rw [Submodule.span_le]
        rintro y hy
        rcases hy with rfl | hy
        · exact hx
        · have hy_V : y ∈ V := Finset.mem_of_mem_erase (by exact_mod_cast hy)
          rw [← hV_span]
          exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hy_V)
      · rw [← hV_span, Submodule.span_le]
        intro v hv
        by_cases hv_eq : v = v₀
        · rw [hv_eq]; exact hv₀_in_new
        · refine Submodule.subset_span ?_
          right
          exact_mod_cast Finset.mem_erase.mpr ⟨hv_eq, by exact_mod_cast hv⟩
    -- Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- First, 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R, and mkx x = 0.
    set mkx : R →+* (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := Ideal.Quotient.mk _ with hmkx_def
    have h_mkx_x : mkx x = 0 :=
      Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x)
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have h𝔪R'_eq_map : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) =
        Ideal.map mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← hcomap_eq]
      exact (Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).symm
    -- Now 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx (span (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --         = Ideal.span (mkx '' (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --         = Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)))
    --         ≤ Submodule.span R' (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- For the spanFinrank bound it suffices to show the inequality
    -- spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |V.erase v₀|.
    have h_bound : Submodule.spanFinrank
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) ≤ V.card - 1 := by
      have h𝔪R'_span : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) =
          Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
        rw [h𝔪R'_eq_map, ← h𝔪R_new, Ideal.map_span,
            Set.image_insert_eq, h_mkx_x]
        -- Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' V.erase v₀)) = Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀)
        rw [Set.insert_eq, Submodule.span_union]
        simp [Submodule.span_singleton_eq_bot.mpr rfl, bot_sup_eq]
      calc Submodule.spanFinrank
            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
          = Submodule.spanFinrank
              (Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R))) := by rw [h𝔪R'_span]
        _ = Submodule.spanFinrank
              (Submodule.span _ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R))) := rfl
        _ ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)).ncard :=
            Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite
              ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet.image _)
        _ ≤ (V.erase v₀ : Set R).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le _
        _ = (V.erase v₀).card := by simp
        _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V
    -- Step 7: conclude using h_n_ge_1.
    omega
